newsSince: On June 11, 2026 NCC Group released H1 FY26 interim results and concluded its strategic review: it ruled out a sale and will remain a listed pure-play cyber security business. The Escode disposal completed May 29 for £262.8m net, leaving ~£230m net cash; the board announced a ~£185m capital return (£170m tender + £15m buyback, after an earlier £40m buyback). Cyber revenue grew 5.9% cc to £118.4m, group adjusted EBITDA +27.7%, record cyber gross margin 38.4%, three consecutive quarters of cyber growth. The stock fell ~13% as the sale process ended, but the underlying pure-play cyber thesis and balance sheet strengthened.

sources.label: Conclusion of the Cyber Strategic Review (Investegate)
NCC concludes strategic review, remains listed (MarketScreener)

verdicts.rankRationale: H1 FY26 (Jun 11): cyber rev +5.9%, EBITDA +27.7%, Escode sold for GBP262.8m -> pure-play cyber-assurance with ~GBP230m net cash + GBP185m returns. Modestly strengthens the IT-security choke-point thesis this watchlist rewards. -12.9% move is sell-the-news, not thesis damage; hold rank, small fundamental uptick.
verdicts.bull: Pure-play cyber pivot complete; three straight quarters of cyber growth, EBITDA margins +3.2pts, fortress balance sheet (~GBP230m net cash) funding GBP185m of returns.
verdicts.neutral: Solid execution but small-cap (~$0.5B) and assurance/testing is only loosely a scaling/test-time bottleneck; FY guidance only mid-to-low single-digit.
verdicts.bear: Post-disposal NCC is a shrunken pure-play with mid-single-digit growth; -12.9% drop signals the market doubts the re-rate, and AI-scaling relevance is tenuous.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Pure-play cyber re-rating + GBP185m capital return execution
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyber demand softens; thin AI-scaling linkage caps multiple
